rationale: Both papers center on abstract vs. concrete construal/mental representation, and the cited JMR study provides a process account (perceived similarity, comparison intensity, choice difficulty) that is plausibly a theoretical source for the citing paper’s construal-matching framework. Given the strong construct overlap in the notes and a confirmed DOI-level reference match (10.1509/jmr.10.0390), this is not a clearly incidental background mention.
